name: Tutorial / How-To
description: Search-first 3-step tutorial / how-to. 45-60s vertical 9:16, optimized for YouTube Shorts (search-first king) but exports cleanly to TikTok and Reels. Power-word hook (Free / Banned / Secret / $1) at 0-2s, foreshadowing of the payoff at 2-5s, three named-transition steps (Hoyos: 'Let's get cooking') from 5-50s, emotional payoff at 50-60s. Targets a 90% retention rate via tight pacing, on-screen step counters, and a single open loop carried from hook to payoff.

stackSummary.keyframes: google/gemini-3-pro-image-preview via OpenRouter — generates the talking-head opener, three step keyframes (one per beat), and the payoff frame. Multi-ref when the user supplies a tool / product / brand reference.
stackSummary.i2v: kwaivgi/kling-v3.0-pro via OpenRouter, 5-7s clips per scene (talking-head hook + 3 step shots + payoff). generate_audio: false. Hands-in-frame and tight close-ups for steps; eye-contact medium for hook and payoff.
stackSummary.voiceover: ElevenLabs eleven_multilingual_v2. Single voice, energetic-but-warm tone. Hoyos-style cadence: short sentences, no filler. Two takes (hook+steps and payoff line) so the payoff can sit slightly slower.
stackSummary.music: ElevenLabs Music — one upbeat search-tutorial bed (~110-130 BPM, light percussion, no vocals). Subtle duck under VO. Optional rising fill at the step-3 → payoff transition.
stackSummary.captions: OpenRouter whisper-1 transcription, then HormoziCaptions for emphasis on the hook word and step labels. Always burn-in: 85% of mobile views are muted.
stackSummary.stepCards: Remotion-rendered text overlays — large step counter (1/3, 2/3, 3/3) top-left, step label center-bottom, named-transition card ('Let's get cooking', 'Now the magic', 'Here's the trick') as a 0.5s full-frame wipe between steps.

constants.structure: 0-2s power-word hook → 2-5s foreshadow → 5-50s steps 1/2/3 with named transitions → 50-60s emotional payoff
constants.foreshadowWindow: 2-5s, shows the payoff but does not reveal the steps
constants.payoffWindow: 50-60s, slow down 15% on the final beat

variationAxes: niche (cooking, productivity, AI tools, finance, beauty hacks, fitness, parenting, DIY, software shortcuts, money-saving)
step count (3 default; 5 acceptable for dense niches like AI workflows or recipes — never 2, never >5)
face vs faceless (creator-on-camera vs hands-only B-roll with VO; AI avatar acceptable as the third option)
platform cut (Shorts 60s default / TikTok 30-45s tighter / Reels 45s mid)
hook category (free-cheap / secret-hidden / contrarian / urgency / list / niche-specific — see hooks.md)
payoff style (visual reveal / first-bite / before-after / number / emotional reaction)
VO language (English default; works in any language — the search-first benefit is language-bound, so pick deliberately)
doNotCopyLiterally: Topic, exact 3 steps, hook wording, payoff line, on-screen text. Vibe and structure only — copy the cadence and the named-transition discipline, not the words.
referenceNotes: Reference is OPTIONAL but strongly recommended when the tutorial demonstrates a specific real product, tool, app UI, branded ingredient, or named person. Without a reference for those, the model will hallucinate logos and UIs. For generic tutorials ('how to chop an onion', 'how to write a better prompt'), no reference is needed — generate freely. The reference-required gate (AGENTS.md hard rule #3) still applies if the brief names a brand.
